summary: Set 047 Session 5 shipped the writer-flip phase part 2 of the v4 schema migration: all four TypeScript writer surfaces — synthesizeNotStartedState / notStartedPayload, ensureSessionStateFile / backfillPayload, cancelSessionSet, restoreSessionSet — now emit canonical v4 on-disk shape per spec section 3.1, mirroring the Python S4 flip byte-for-byte on the v4 contract. The new helper toV4OnDiskShape in cancelLifecycle.ts projects any v1/v2/v3/v4 input through the normalizeToV4Shape shim and trims to the v4 contract, preserving the plan-less carve-out (absent sessions[] + top-level orchestrator/startedAt passthrough) across cancel/restore — identical semantic to Python session_lifecycle._to_v4_on_disk_shape. notStartedPayload's readTotalSessionsFromSpec falls back on ### Session N headings when the config block lacks a numeric totalSessions (mirrors Python S4 verifier Critical 2). backfillPayload writes per-session startedAt onto sessions[0] for the activity-log branch and promotes all sessions to status: complete for the change-log branch, leaving per-session completedAt at null (the change-log mtime is a set-level heuristic, not a per-session boundary — matches Python parity). Explorer prerequisites surface (spec §3.3): new SessionSetPrerequisite type, SessionSet.prerequisites field parsed from spec.md, SessionSet.blockedByPrereqs derived via the new deriveBlockedByPrereqs helper (runs in readSessionSets per-root AND in readAllSessionSets against the merged map so cross-root prereq resolution works — S5 verifier Important-2 fix), parsePrerequisites lightweight regex parser tolerant of YAML inline comments and operator typos (S5 verifier Important-1 fix), blockedByPrereqsBadge predicate suppressed on terminal-state rows, contextValueFor gating blocked-by-prereqs on non-terminal state. Cross-provider verification ran one round against gpt-5-4 tier 3 ($0.4717) and returned ISSUES_FOUND with 0 Critical + 2 Important + 2 Nice-to-have items; all 4 items addressed in-flight per memory feedback_dont_hide_behind_out_of_scope: parsePrerequisites condition handling distinguishes 'absent → default complete' from 'present-but-bad → drop' with YAML inline-comment stripping, blockedByPrereqs cross-reference factored into deriveBlockedByPrereqs and re-invoked at the merged-list boundary, explicit sessions: [] preservation pinned by new regression test, terminal-row badge suppression added as Layer-3 scenario 3. 13 new TS tests in sessionStateV4Writers.test.ts + 12 new in prerequisites.test.ts + 4 Layer-3 Playwright scenarios in blocked-by-prereqs.spec.ts + 13-item UAT checklist. Final: 623 TS unit tests pass (vs 553 pre-S5 baseline) with 2 pre-existing baseline failures unchanged; 896 Python tests pass + 1 skipped + 0 failed. Cumulative S1+S2+S3+S4+S5 routed cost: $1.809 of $10 NTE (18.1%). 13 new TS tests in sessionStateV4Writers.test.ts.

reason.specifics: Set 047 Session 6 is the schema-doc + authoring-guide revision + close-out + publish session per the scope-locked spec.md section 4: rewrite docs/session-state-schema.md to canonical v4, update docs/planning/session-set-authoring-guide.md to document the prerequisites field, generate change-log.md for the set, run a final cross-provider verification of the bundled set, and dual-publish (dabbler-ai-router minor bump + extension Marketplace minor bump). docs/adoption-bootstrap.md revision is DEFERRED to Set 048 per spec section 4. Claude Opus 4.7 high-effort is the right pick — the same orchestrator that closed S1-S5 knows the v4 contract end-to-end, and S6 is a docs-and-release wrap-up where consistency of voice matters more than fresh perspective.
